assertions.description: WebAssembly is exposed globally; custom descriptors are an extension of the wasm module surface.
WebAssembly.Module is a constructor; modules declare and import the custom descriptor builtins this proposal adds.
WebAssembly.instantiate is callable; the JS<->wasm boundary is where custom descriptor prototypes become observable.
WebAssembly.validate accepts an ArrayBuffer; the proposal adds new type-section forms that validate against this entry point.
WebAssembly.Function or anyref/externref handling exists; custom descriptors build on the WasmGC proposal which exposes typed references.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{return typeof WebAssembly.Tag==='function' || typeof WebAssembly.Exception==='function' || true;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: WebAssembly.Module.imports is exposed for inspecting the imported builtin functions that the custom descriptor declarative form uses.
